ogTitle: Selbourne vs Seven Mile Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Selbourne and Seven Mile Beach.
heading: Selbourne vs Seven Mile Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Selbourne, Tasmania and Seven Mile Beach, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Seven Mile Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Selbourne (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Selbourne or Seven Mile Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Selbourne has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Seven Mile Beach scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Selbourne scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Seven Mile Beach scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Selbourne or Seven Mile Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Seven Mile Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.92% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
